790.reason: [The lookahead logic is incorrect: it assumes that FuncDef starts with something followed by '(' and that MainFuncDef can be detected by looking for MAINTK as the next token, but this fails to properly distinguish between VarDecl (which starts with INTTK/CHARTK) and FuncDef (which also starts with INTTK/CHARTK/VOIDTK). This causes valid function definitions to be misparsed as declarations or skipped entirely.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [The lookahead logic is incorrect: it assumes that FuncDef starts with something followed by '(' and that MainFuncDef can be detected by looking for MAINTK as the next token, but this fails to properly distinguish between VarDecl (which starts with INTTK/CHARTK) and FuncDef (which also starts with INTTK/CHARTK/VOIDTK). This causes valid function definitions to be misparsed as declarations or skipped entirely.]

798.reason: [This condition is flawed: it tries to parse FuncDef until it sees MAINTK, but the lookahead of 1 token is insufficient. A FuncDef starts with FuncType (VOIDTK/INTTK/CHARTK) followed by IDENFR and then '(', so checking only the next token against MAINTK doesn't correctly identify where FuncDefs end and MainFuncDef begins. This can cause the parser to either consume the main function as a regular function or fail to parse functions properly.]
